div1.entryFree: Preposterously, perversely, against the natural order of things: that it (my nature) could so p. be stained, to leave for nothing all thy sum of good, Sonn. 109, 11. methinks you prescribe to yourself very p. Wiv. II, 2, 250. those things do best please me that befal p. Mids. III, 2, 121. whatsoever cunning fiend it was that wrought upon thee so p. H5 II, 2, 112. for nature so p. to err, Oth. I, 3, 62.
